zh.lifeSummary: 當Agnes Samon於1503年出生在英國英格蘭賀特福爾比曉普也斯 斯托特福德時，她的父親Robert Samon43歲，母親Margaret Rice27歲。她和Dafydd Ap Llywelyn至少有4個兒子和2個女兒。她去世於1554年在她的家鄉享年51歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭賀特福爾比曉普也斯 斯托特福德。

nameMeanings.name: Samon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish (Mayo): variant of   Salmon  .

variants.name: Salmon
meanings: English and French: from the Middle English and Old French personal name Salmon, Saumon, a shortened form of Salomon (see Solomon ). The Normans introduced it to England, and it was current as a personal name there until at least the end of the 14th century. The derived surname is found throughout 13th- and 14th-century England, mostly in the spellings Saleman and Salman, which eventually became regularized as Salmon. See also 2 below, compare Salman 3.

English: occasionally perhaps a nickname for someone ‘as wise as Solomon’. In Chaucer's Canon's Yeoman's Tale, it is stated: ‘And whan we been togidres euerichoon, Euery man semeth a Salomon’. King Solomon was famed for his wisdom.
English: nickname from Anglo-Norman French, Middle English salmon, saumo(u)n, saumun, samoun ‘salmon’, no doubt given to someone who caught, sold, or ate the fish.

variants.name: Sammons
meanings: English: variant of Sammon (see Salmon ), with post-medieval excrescent -s.

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

en.lifeSummary: When Agnes Samon was born in 1503, in Bishop's Stortford, Hertfordshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Robert Samon, was 43 and her mother, Margaret Rice, was 27. She had at least 4 sons and 2 daughters with Dafydd Ap Llywelyn. She died in 1554, in her hometown, at the age of 51, and was buried in Bishop's Stortford, Hertfordshire, England, United Kingdom.

ko.lifeSummary: Agnes Samon(이)가 1503년 영국 잉글랜드 허트퍼드실르 비숍'스 스토르트퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Robert Samon(은)는 43세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret Rice(은)는 27세였다. 그녀는 Dafydd Ap Llywelyn(와)과 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1554년 그녀의 고향에서 51세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 허트퍼드실르 비숍'스 스토르트퍼드에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Agnes Samon родилась в 1503 г. в местности бишоп'с стортфорд, хиртфордшир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Robert Samon было 43 года, а матери, которую звали Margaret Rice, 27. У неё было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери от Dafydd Ap Llywelyn. Она умерла в 1554 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 51 год и была похоронена в местности бишоп'с стортфорд, хиртфордшир, Англия, Великобритания.
